factors.name: AI Semiconductor Demand Surge
factors.summary: Samsung expected to post 18-fold profit jump on AI memory demand (per Bloomberg). SK Hynix planning $29B US listing to compete in AI chip market; TSMC Citi upgrade signals 2026 guidance raise on AI demand. Memory pricing strength validates earnings momentum vs. speculation.

factors.name: Hyperscaler Capex Momentum
factors.summary: Anthropic committing $15B USD for 1.4-GW Australian data centre capacity (AFR), signaling sustained cloud/AI infrastructure investment. Foxconn Q2 revenue surges on AI boost despite geopolitical warnings. Forward guidance on capex will be critical watch ahead of earnings.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Easing
factors.summary: Gold prices rising and rate-hike bets cooling (per Bloomberg Markets). Reduced expectations for Fed hikes support long-duration growth multiples critical to NAS100. Real yields have likely compressed, benefiting tech valuations.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran-US Deal Holds)
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening continues; tankers transiting via US-protected corridor. Oil prices collapsing on overstocking (per TotalEnergies CEO), reducing stagflation risk and supporting risk-on sentiment. De-escalation trades favor equities.

factors.name: China Macro Softness & Yuan Weakness
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY reference at 6.8066 vs prior 6.7780, signaling yuan weakness. China's slower domestic growth and Beijing signalling openness to foreign capital suggest growth concerns. Heavy NAS100 exposure to China-dependent tech could drag.

factors.name: Asian Chip Stock Pullback After Rebound
factors.summary: Japan, South Korea chipmakers pulling back after recent rebound (per MarketWatch). Samsung/SK Hynix leveraged ETF volatility warnings from Bank of Korea signal potential regulatory tightening and profit-taking risk.

factors.name: Short-Term Momentum & Valuation Stretch
factors.summary: Strategist warns momentum trades face violent unwind risk in July (MarketWatch). NAS100 +0.44% in 4h after -1.24% 24h suggests choppy price action. Mega-cap tech multiples already priced for AI growth; limited margin of safety.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Real Yields Neutral-to-Firm
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 161.70, JGB 10-yr yields 2.79% (30-yr high). Euro consolidating below 1.1400. If real yields stabilize or tick higher, long-duration growth stocks face headwind; if yields continue lower, equities rallied harder.

factors.name: Limited Near-Term Data Catalyst
factors.summary: No major CPI, Fed minutes, or employment data in immediate 24-72h window. Bond auctions (10/30Y Treasuries) this week are moderate-risk. Earnings season still ramping; NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL/AAPL dates critical but not imminent.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two competing narratives: (1) genuine AI semiconductor earnings momentum (Samsung 18x profit jump, TSMC upgrade, hyperscaler capex acceleration) and (2) extended momentum rally facing profit-taking and geopolitical/macro headwinds. The recent 4h +0.44% bounce off a 24h -1.24% decline suggests choppy consolidation rather than directional conviction. De-escalation of Iran tensions and cooling rate-hike expectations provide tailwinds, but China's Yuan weakness, Asian chip stock pullbacks, and July seasonality warnings flag caution. Mega-cap tech valuations remain stretched, and sentiment-driven reversals in momentum trades could unwind quickly ahead of earnings season.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, AMZN earnings (mid-to-late July)
Samsung earnings confirmation of memory profit beat (July 8-9)
US jobs report (early July if scheduled)
Any escalation in Strait of Hormuz tensions or geopolitical flashpoint
ECB communications on rate path (Nagel, Moulin commentary watch)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed minutes signal renewed hawkishness or inflation resilience, real yields could spike, crushing long-duration growth multiples and triggering NAS100 selloff. Conversely, if mega-cap earnings disappoint on AI capex outlook or demand slowdown signals, even dovish Fed support may not sustain rally. China stimulus reversal, Taiwan tensions escalation, or renewed Hormuz disruption are geopolitical wildcards. Momentum unwind into July could be violent—watch single-stock gamma in NVDA/MSFT/AAPL for dealer position shifts.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 with mixed signals. Wait for pullback to 29100 support or confirmation break above 29600 resistance before committing size. Given earnings-driven volatility ahead and extended 4h positioning, scale in rather than chase. Watch Fed minutes release for directional clarity.

factors.name: Nvidia AI chip delay (SemiAnalysis)
factors.summary: Next-gen AI server rack delayed to 2028 due to manufacturing snags, raising concerns about Nvidia's annual release cadence hitting manufacturing limits. Critical headwind for NDX's heaviest AI darling.

factors.name: Dovish repricing post-NFP (soft payrolls)
factors.summary: Last week's mixed June NFP triggered dovish repricing; July Fed hike odds fell to 24%, September to 55%. Lower rate expectations typically support long-duration growth multiples in Nasdaq.

factors.name: Tech earnings season imminent (Samsung, NVDA next)
factors.summary: Samsung results Tuesday are described as 'high-stakes' validation for AI trade; NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings coming this week/next. Outcome could swing NDX 2–3%+ either direction.

factors.name: Real yields (DXY, USD strength)
factors.summary: DXY holding near 101.00; USD/JPY rallying to 162.20 on rate-hike expectations despite soft jobs data. Stronger dollar and sticky real yields create headwind for long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Oil glut fears, OPEC+ raise output
factors.summary: Oil down >1% post-OPEC+ agreement to raise output; oil glut concerns rising. Typically supports growth multiples via lower energy costs, but geopolitical risk (Hormuz, China missile test) adds noise.

factors.name: FOMC minutes due this week
factors.summary: Markets await FOMC minutes as major catalyst for Fed narrative and rate expectations. Could repricing real yields sharply—timing and surprise direction unknown.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech positioning & gamma risk
factors.summary: No specific data on dealer gamma or options flows in news; however, earnings season concentration risk is extremely high—single stock beats/misses can whipsaw NQ 1–2%.

factors.name: CPI report due Tuesday (next day)
factors.summary: US CPI data is high-impact catalyst scheduled for next Tuesday; currently pricing in core PCE expectations. Inflation surprise either direction would override current dovish repricing.

factors.name: 4h/24h price action—modest moves
factors.summary: 4h change +0.44%, 24h change -1.24%—small consolidation range, no strong directional commitment. Markets in wait-and-see mode into earnings and FOMC.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq 100 is consolidating after last week's strong rally (+largest weekly gain since early May per Deutsche Bank) driven by dovish repricing post-soft NFP. However, the rally is now colliding with two major headwinds: (1) Nvidia's reported AI server delay to 2028 undermines confidence in the near-term AI capex super-cycle, and (2) real yields remain sticky as the USD holds firm near 101.00. This week's earnings (Samsung Tue, NVDA/MSFT/AAPL soon after) and FOMC minutes are binary catalysts that could reignite momentum or trigger a pullback. Current price action is directionless—neither extension of the rally nor a reversal has gained traction.
catalysts_ahead: Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Google earnings (likely mid-late week)
screening.risk_warning: If CPI comes in hot (>3.5% headline, >3.2% core), dovish repricing unwinds and real yields spike—expect sharp NDX selloff and move toward support. If Samsung/NVDA earnings disappoint on demand or guidance, AI narrative cracks and leadership rotates out of mega-cap tech. Conversely, if FOMC minutes appear more dovish or earnings beat with strong guidance, resistance at 29,600+ breaks and rally resumes.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is incremental and directionless; avoid chasing. Wait for either confirmation above 29,600 on earnings beat + dovish catalyst, or retest of support near 29,150 for counter-trend long. CPI and FOMC minutes are imminent—cap position size into these events.

factors.name: Tech futures strength into open
factors.summary: Nasdaq 100 futures advanced 1.03% to ~29,860 during European trading (per FXStreet 08:34 UTC), signaling a tech-led rebound bid to start the week after Friday's -1.24% slide.

factors.name: AI capex tailwinds / hyperscaler rotation narrative
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley (12:09 UTC) flags AI hyperscalers poised for 'big comeback rally' as semiconductor trade cools. SK Hynix $28B US listing this week seen as AI memory demand signal. TeraWulf +20% on Anthropic data center lease highlights capex acceleration.

factors.name: Oil price collapse / macro tailwind
factors.summary: WTI down to $68.30 (-0.64%), Saudi Arabia made largest crude cut in 26 years; Evercore ISI notes oil exit from 'danger zone' is tailwind for economy and equity multiples. Reduces stagflation fears.

factors.name: USD strength
factors.summary: DXY retook 101.00 (FXStreet 09:06 UTC), USD/JPY at ~162.30 near 40-year highs. Stronger dollar typically headwind for NQ international revenue exposure and reduces relative attractiveness of US growth multiples abroad.

factors.name: Real yields / duration sensitivity unclear
factors.summary: No fresh 10Y Treasury or TIPS move reported in news window. Last week's NFP was dovish (July hike odds at 24%, September at 55%), which should support growth multiples, but data dependency into CPI week (likely Wed/Thu) keeps positioning fluid.

factors.name: Crypto / risk-on sentiment mixed
factors.summary: Bitcoin bounced to best week since March, but Michael Saylor's Strategy sold record 3,588 BTC ($216M) to fund dividends—potential liquidation headwind. Crypto strength historically supports NQ, but forced selling is a caution.

factors.name: Quant rotation / momentum fade
factors.summary: Bloomberg (11:55 UTC) reports quant hedge funds extending worst run since 2023 as momentum slides; violent rotation in underbelly of bull market suggests positioning fragility and potential for sharp reversals.

factors.name: Semis / NVDA outlook uncertain
factors.summary: ASML target raised at Bernstein on capex cycle intensity, but no fresh NVDA guidance or earnings news. Samsung reportedly proposing 20% ASP hike signals tight supply but also margin pressure risk.

factors.name: CPI data imminent (likely mid-week)
factors.summary: No CPI print in news window yet; critical inflation data due soon. Current market repricing dovish after NFP, but a CPI beat could quickly flip rate expectations and compress multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical noise (NATO summit, Iran/Hormuz, Ukraine)
factors.summary: Trump heading to NATO summit, Ukraine strike/Russia barrage, Iran control of Strait of Hormuz remain live. Tanker traffic restored, but geopolitical premium remains; unlikely major NQ driver unless escalation.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is rebounding early Monday on the back of a dovish NFP repricing and rotation narrative from growth-heavy tech to AI hyperscalers. Oil's sharp pullback removes stagflation tail-risk, and SK Hynix IPO / Anthropic capex signals reinvigorate the AI infrastructure story. However, the move is already ~1% into futures and price is only +0.44% in 4h—phase 2 territory. Real-yield sensitivity remains acute into this week's CPI data, and quant-fund momentum fade plus MSTR's forced BTC liquidations suggest positioning fragility.
catalysts_ahead: CPI / Core CPI release (likely Wed or Thu this week) — critical for Fed repricing and multiple re-rating
SK Hynix US ADR listing (expected this week) — $28B IPO, major AI capex signal
ISM Services PMI (likely later this week or next) — gauges services inflation and margin outlook
Treasury curve repricing into CPI — watch 10Y yield moves for real-yield impact on NQ multiples
FOMC meeting (late July or August) — forward guidance on terminal rate remain uncertain given NFP miss
screening.risk_warning: Move is already underway (+1% in futures); CPI miss (hotter inflation) could trigger sharp multiple compression and reversal. Quant momentum fade + MSTR liquidations suggest leverage is weak; any sharp reversal in risk sentiment (geopolitical shock, banking stress) could cascade. USD strength and real-yield re-pricing (if CPI surprises hot) would be immediate headwinds.
screening.entry_guidance: Move underway in phase 2; avoid chasing breakouts. Wait for retest of support ~29,100–29,200 or for CPI print clarity before establishing fresh longs. If CPI data delays, watch for consolidation near 29,300–29,400 before committing.
